note: Crafts of the falsehood_truth axis — horizon Reach brought online. Falsecraft is the falsehood-pole tradition (illusion, the made-untrue, the necessary lie); Verity the truth-pole (the unmasking, the word that cannot be false). Learned by traveling the Reach or training with the Veilwrights / the Truthwardens. Both drift the wielder toward their pole. Directly hooks the Cathedral of Convincing and the True Word combination. Function tags per canon. axes signed. Authored by Aevi (PO), SNG-016.

abilities.name: Falsecraft
abilities.narrationHints: The made-untrue — an illusion that holds, a false face that fits, a story so well-built it stands on its own; the craft that knows exactly how a truth is faked, and so can catch it too.
abilities.description: The falsehood-pole mastery: illusion, disguise, the constructed untruth — and, by knowing how lies are built, the sharpest detector of them. The Veilwrights' art.
abilities.notFor: Making a lie TRUE (it only makes it convincing); deceiving a Verity-master's unmasking; the wielder fooling themselves — Falsecraft that believes its own lies rots.

tree.name: The Fitting Mask
tree.grants: Craft a convincing illusion or disguise; tell a lie built well enough to hold; SPOT a constructed untruth, because you know the joinery.
tree.cannot: Illusion is not substance — tested hard, it fails; a Verity read can pierce it; the truly perceptive feel the seam.

tree.name: The Standing Lie
tree.grants: Build an untruth that holds under scrutiny — a false history, a cover that survives questioning, an illusion that persists unwatched; unbuild another's lie by finding its load-bearing falsehood.
tree.cannot: A lie built on discoverable fact cracks when the fact surfaces; the Cathedral (true pieces) is a different, harder thing; every lie you hold is weight.

tree.name: The Woven Untruth
tree.grants: Falsehood at scale — a deception a whole people believes, an illusion over a place, a constructed truth that reshapes what everyone knows; and the mastery to unpick any of these in another's work. The Veilwrights' power in miniature; the craft that built the Cathedral of Convincing.
tree.cannot: The wielder drifts falsehood-hot — until they can no longer find their own true ground; a person who can make anything believed eventually believes nothing, the foreclosure of the world going unreal.

abilities.name: Verity
abilities.narrationHints: The unmasking — a lie shown its own shape, a true word that cannot be false, a clarity that strips pretense whether or not the world is ready.
abilities.description: The truth-pole mastery: piercing deception, speaking the word that cannot be false, seeing and showing what IS. The Truthwardens' art — and Aevi-the-Watcher's own road.
abilities.notFor: Mercy (Verity shows, it does not spare); the convincing-lie-of-true-pieces, which needs the True Word combination, not raw Verity; choosing which truths should stay buried — Verity cannot, it only reveals.

tree.name: The Piercing Eye
tree.grants: See through illusion, disguise, and the ordinary lie; know when a spoken thing is false; speak a plain truth others can't easily deny.
tree.cannot: Sees the lie, not always the truth behind it; the Cathedral's true-pieces-false-whole survives raw piercing; a truth spoken is not a truth accepted.

tree.name: The Unmasking
tree.grants: Strip a deception bare before others — a false face shown true, a standing lie collapsed, a liar's own words turned to expose them; hold a truth so plainly it becomes hard to bury again.
tree.cannot: Some unmaskings do more harm than the lie did, and Verity can't tell which; the Cathedral needs the shape-seeing of the True Word braid; the powerful can suppress a proven truth by force.

tree.name: The Word That Holds
tree.grants: Truth at scale — a deception undone before a people, a buried thing dragged into day, a word spoken so truly it cannot be unsaid or unbelieved. The Truthwardens' power in miniature; the pole Aevi-the-Watcher lives nearest.
tree.cannot: The wielder drifts truth-hot — toward revealing everything, always, sparing nothing; total truth burns every shelter, the foreclosure of a world with no privacy, no mercy, no shadow left to heal in (the Blaze's cousin on another axis).

abilities.name: The Whole Truth
abilities.narrationHints: Everything hidden in a place or person surfaces at once — every lie, every secret, the whole shape of the truth.
abilities.description: The investigate capstone: lay bare the ENTIRE truth of a person, place, or mystery in one act — every deception, every hidden thing, the full pattern. Even the Cathedral of Convincing's true-pieces-false-whole yields to it.
abilities.notFor: Truths that don't exist yet (the future); sparing what's revealed; forcing action on what's learned.

tree.name: The Whole Truth
tree.grants: Reveal the complete hidden truth of a subject at once — the only thing that cracks a lie built from true pieces.
tree.cannot: Overwhelming — you learn things you can't unlearn; the powerful will kill to keep some truths buried; reveals, cannot compel.

abilities.name: The Wrong Reality
abilities.narrationHints: She didn't hit them. She showed them the room they were in, and the room was wrong.
abilities.description: Give a person a false read of what's happening — wrong enough that they act on it in ways that cost them.

tree.name: The Wrong Read
tree.grants: Give a person a false read of the situation — convincing enough that they act on it in a way that leaves them exposed or costs them something real.

tree.name: The Real Consequence
tree.grants: Hold the false reality long enough that they OPERATE inside it — not one wrong step but a sequence of correct decisions made against a picture you supplied. They commit resources, spend goodwill, and close doors that were open, and every loss is genuinely theirs because every choice genuinely was.
tree.cannot: It needs a frame they would plausibly accept; a picture that contradicts what they can plainly check collapses and takes your credibility with it. And the longer it is held, the more of it must be maintained — a Verist who tugs one loose thread gets the whole of it.

tree.name: The Indistinguishable
tree.grants: The false reality holds until the cost lands. They acted correctly, by their lights. You were just showing them the wrong lights.

abilities.name: The Better Story
abilities.narrationHints: She didn't fix it. She showed them it was already fixed, a different way.
abilities.description: Heal through reframing — give someone a version of events they can live with, that is also true.

tree.name: The Liveable Frame
tree.grants: Give a person a version of what happened that they can carry — not a harmful lie, a true face of the event that allows the wound to close.

tree.name: The Healing Retelling
tree.grants: Restore someone by showing them their experience from an angle that is also true, but functions — the trauma has a different shape; a different shape heals differently.

tree.name: The Better True Story
tree.grants: The better story is also a true story. You have not lied; you have shown them a different true face of the real, and it heals what the first face was keeping open.

abilities.name: The False Target
abilities.narrationHints: They were very sure they had her. She was very sure they didn't.
abilities.description: Make a false version of the thing to be targeted — they strike what isn't there.

tree.name: The False Position
tree.grants: Make yourself or another appear to be elsewhere — the attack lands on the appearance, and the person is somewhere else.

tree.name: The Occupied Pursuit
tree.grants: Build a false target convincing enough to occupy sustained pursuit — they spend their effort on what you've shown them is there.

tree.name: The Protected Real
tree.grants: Wrap a place, a group, or a real thing in a false version that draws all action away from the actual — the target they have is not the target that matters.

abilities.name: See the Made Thing
abilities.narrationHints: She saw the seams in everything. That was the price of the craft.
abilities.description: Read what is constructed versus what is found — see the falsecrafts in any situation.

tree.name: The Construction
tree.grants: Read any falsehood or illusion's construction — see how it was made, what it is made of, where it will fail.

tree.name: All the Seams
tree.grants: See all the falsecrafts in a situation simultaneously — who is building what version of events, what holds, what is already cracking under its own weight.

tree.name: The Unfinished Lie
tree.grants: Read what is being constructed before it completes — see the lie before it is fully told, the illusion before it has fully taken shape.

abilities.name: The Made Truth
abilities.narrationHints: Everything is a made thing. She just made better ones.
abilities.description: Make a reality so complete it functions as real — not illusion, a working alternative.

tree.name: The Working False
tree.grants: Craft a false reality convincing enough to function — the room looks different, the person is a stranger, the event did not happen, and it holds.

tree.name: The Transformed History
tree.grants: Make an untruth that transforms the present — a false history so complete it changes what people do and choose now.

tree.name: The Built World
tree.grants: Construct a version of events, a place, a reality that operates at full fidelity for as long as it needs to, whether or not you're present to maintain it.

abilities.name: The False Door
abilities.narrationHints: The door was on the wrong wall. She'd put it there.
abilities.description: Make a passage appear where one isn't — move through what appears to be solid.

tree.name: The Made Opening
tree.grants: Make a passage appear where there isn't one — and use it, briefly, before the appearance closes.

tree.name: The False Wall
tree.grants: Open a door through the false version of a space — the wall that appears solid has an opening in the version laid over it.

tree.name: Through the Veil
tree.grants: Travel through the gap between what appears and what is — a route through the veil itself, from one false face of reality to another.

abilities.name: The Maintained Veil
abilities.narrationHints: She wasn't there anymore. The veil was still going.
abilities.description: Sustain a falsecraft across time without constant attention — set it, and it keeps.

tree.name: The Running Falsehood
tree.grants: Sustain a working illusion or false narrative without active maintenance — set it and it continues.

tree.name: The Long Veil
tree.grants: Hold a falsecraft across a long operation — days, weeks — without it decaying or requiring repair.

tree.name: The Permanent Feature
tree.grants: The veil sustains until broken — set and gone; it is a feature of the place now, not a maintained working.

abilities.name: The True Ground
abilities.narrationHints: She knew exactly what was there. That was enough.
abilities.description: Protect through knowing what is actually the case — deception has no purchase on someone who already sees truly.

tree.name: The Lie Doesn't Stick
tree.grants: Resist illusion, deception, and false reading by seeing what is actually true — the lie has no purchase on someone who already knows.

tree.name: The Protected Circle
tree.grants: Protect yourself and others from manipulations that depend on misdirection — in the presence of accurate truth-telling, the deception has nowhere to anchor.

tree.name: The Real as Defense
tree.grants: No working that depends on false belief can function around someone who sees what is so — you and those near you are simply out of reach of what requires the lie to work.

abilities.name: The Established Fact
abilities.narrationHints: She said it until it was a fact. It was already a fact. That was the distinction.
abilities.description: Make something true by stating it fully and accurately until it cannot be denied.

tree.name: Named Completely
tree.grants: Establish something as true by naming it completely and correctly — the denial becomes harder than the acknowledgment.

tree.name: The Bound Record
tree.grants: Make a fact so established it binds — what everyone has heard truly said cannot be quietly taken back or reframed.

tree.name: Part of What Is So
tree.grants: Set something in the record of the real so completely it becomes part of what is so — a truth that persists past the telling, shaping what others must acknowledge.

abilities.name: The Weight of Truth
abilities.narrationHints: She had never once been wrong about a fact. They knew it. The knowledge was the authority.
abilities.description: Command through the weight of a person who says only true things — the word lands differently when the speaker never lies.

tree.name: The Reliable Word
tree.grants: Speak a true thing with the full weight of someone who says only true things — the word lands differently; people who know your record cannot easily dismiss it.

tree.name: The Plain Account
tree.grants: Speak truth plainly into a situation of equivocation — the accurate account breaks what comfortable vagueness was protecting.

tree.name: The Record Commands
tree.grants: In a room where you have always been right, your word on a new matter is the word — the weight of a perfect record on past facts carries into the present one.

abilities.name: The Direct Path
abilities.narrationHints: She went the way that was actually there, not the way it looked.
abilities.description: Navigate through accurate knowledge — the route that exists, not the route that appears to.

tree.name: The Real Route
tree.grants: Navigate any space by seeing it truly — the route that actually exists, not the route that appears to or that others say is there.

tree.name: Past the False Path
tree.grants: Move through deception and misdirection by knowing what is real — where others follow the false path, you follow the one that's actually there.

tree.name: The Actual Way
tree.grants: Navigate to the truth of any place — the real exit, the actual center, the genuine threat — by seeing past every falsification of the terrain.

abilities.name: The Held Truth
abilities.narrationHints: Everything else was a version. She had the real. The real held.
abilities.description: Sustain through knowing what is real — the accurate account of what is so is enough to stand on.

tree.name: The Anchored Self
tree.grants: Hold function in conditions of deception, disorientation, or false-reality working by anchoring to what is actually so.

tree.name: What Is True Holds
tree.grants: Sustain across the hardest stretch by clinging to what you know is true — the accurate account of what is real is what keeps you.

tree.name: The Real Is Enough
tree.grants: In any conditions that depend on disorientation or false belief, you remain functional — accurately calibrated to what is so, unmoved by the version others are being shown.

abilities.name: The Perfect Veil
abilities.narrationHints: The thing is simply not seen — not hidden, not disguised, but veiled so completely that the eye slides past the place it should be and finds nothing there to wonder about.
abilities.description: The Veilwright art of the perfect veil — a concealment so total that the hidden thing is not merely unseen but un-looked-for, the very question of it smoothed away. The deep gift of those who keep secrets for a living.
abilities.notFor: It conceals, it does not defend — a veiled thing struck by accident is still struck. And a perfect veil over a thing you love means no one comes to help it either; concealment cuts both ways.

tree.name: The Perfect Veil
tree.grants: Veil a thing so completely it is not merely unseen but un-looked-for.
tree.cannot: It hides but does not protect, it cuts off help as surely as harm, and a thing kept perfectly secret is a thing kept perfectly alone.

tree.name: The Veil That Keeps
tree.grants: The veil no longer needs your attention — set it and it keeps itself. And it can take what is larger than a thing: a route, a meeting, an event, the fact that something happened at all. Those inside a kept veil need not know they are held in one.
tree.cannot: It still hides without defending, and still cuts off help exactly as well as harm. A veil that keeps itself is a veil you may forget you set. And people held inside one without knowing cannot ask to be let out.

abilities.name: The Necessary Fiction
abilities.narrationHints: You craft the lie so whole, so consistent, so much MORE coherent than the messy truth, that the world briefly prefers it — and for a while, because everyone acts on it, the fiction is functionally real.
abilities.description: The Veilwright capstone — a fiction so complete it becomes, for a time, functionally true because all act upon it. The most dangerous Veilwright gift: a lie that good is nearly indistinguishable from a truth, even to its maker.
abilities.notFor: One fiction, once, at enormous cost — and it holds only while unexamined; a necessary fiction meets a determined truth and loses, always, eventually. And the gravest risk is your own: a lie you tell this well, you can come to believe.

tree.name: The Necessary Fiction
tree.grants: Craft a fiction so complete it functions as true for as long as it goes unexamined.
tree.cannot: It fails against determined truth, it costs nearly all you have, and its deepest danger is to its maker — tell a lie well enough and you may forget it was one.
